Note.Para: Any function, program or document which operates normally may be used.

ListItem.Para: At main base, flight crews request 02 back-up iPads for international flights.
If one Class 3 OPT is unserviceable, all the required OPT announcements and crosschecks are to be made with both crew members by viewing the remaining device.

If navigation charts are not available on one Class 3 device, ensure current charts are available on back-up iPad. iPad is not to be used during critical phases, but may be referenced at other times. Having the required chart(s) displayed on only one Class 3 device is acceptable, but for complex procedures consider using the flight deck printer to produce a hard copy of the relevant chart (for the affected pilot) prior to commencement of the procedure.
Request assistances from dispatcher/ representative office if required.

Note.Para: Any function, program or document which operates normally may be used provided that its database are up to date.

ListItem.Para: At main base, flight crews request 02 back-up iPads for international flights.
Both EDB applications can be unserviceable provided that at least one back-up iPad with updated documents is available.
Both OPT applications can be unserviceable provided that:
At least one back-up iPad with updated 787 RTOW charts are available. All the crosschecks are to be made with both crew members viewing the back-up iPad; or
Flight crew can contact OCC dispatch and provide actual data for all performance calculations.

Both LIDO applications can be unserviceable. For navigation charts, the required departure, destination and/or planned alternate terminal procedures, en-route charts are covered by an back-up iPad. Flight crews can request supports from VNA Office to print out all required charts.
Airport Moving Map: Alternate source of information is not required.
If both Flight Deck Entry Video Surveillance are unserviceable, Refer to MEL item 23-75-01-01 and 23-75-01-02 for Alternate procedures.
If both Class 3 Hardware are inoperative or Class 3 EFB applications are not met operational requirements. Flight crews can operate Class 1 EFB provided that their data are up-to-date.
Request assistances from dispatcher/ representative office if required.
